label: ROOT__

label: Folder
desc: A generic helper to reference folders inside doc files

label: Module
desc: A generic helper to reference modules inside doc files

label: Export
desc: A generic helper to reference exports inside doc files

label: Term
desc: A generic helper to reference terms inside doc files

label: File
desc: A generic helper to reference non-JS/TS files (README.md, yml configs, etc.) inside doc files

label: ref
desc: A placeholder for referencing anything from the global tskb registry into jsx documentation tags

label: build
desc: Builds the knowledge graph from .tskb.tsx sources.

label: search
desc: Searches the graph and returns ranked matches.

label: pick
desc: Returns full details for one node, by ID or path.

label: ls
desc: Lists folders in the graph as a hierarchy.

label: Doc
desc: The root tag of a doc file. Answers one question via its `explains` prop.

label: H1
desc: Heading level 1.

label: H2
desc: Heading level 2.

label: H3
desc: Heading level 3.

label: P
desc: Paragraph.

label: List
desc: List container.

label: Li
desc: List item.

label: Snippet
desc: A type-checked code example.

label: Adr
desc: An Architecture Decision Record.

label: ref
desc: Placeholder for declaring a typed reference to a registered node.

label: Relation
desc: A labeled edge between two registered nodes.

label: DocPriority
desc: How important a doc is: `essential`, `constraint`, or `supplementary`.

label: Flow
desc: A named, ordered sequence of steps through the system.

label: Step
desc: One step inside a Flow, pointing at a registered node.

label: transformGraph
desc: Builds the explorer chunks from the graph.

label: sanitizeFolderId
desc: Turns a folder id into a safe chunk file name.

label: showNodeSpinner
desc: Shows a small spinner next to a node.

label: removeNodeSpinner
desc: Removes a spinner shown by `showNodeSpinner`.

label: DocPanel
desc: Side panel shell. Subscribes to the router and delegates rendering entirely to the active view.

label: Router
desc: Panel router class. Manages a view stack and optional location.hash sync.

label: registerView
desc: Registers a view class and its deps as a factory for hash restoration. The factory is only used when restoring a view from a URL fragment — normal navigation calls push() directly.

label: init
desc: Optionally starts location.hash sync and restores any view encoded in the current hash.

label: push
desc: Pushes a view instance onto the stack, notifies subscribers, and writes the route to the URL hash. Skips identical-route pushes.

label: back
desc: Pops the top view off the stack, notifies subscribers, and rewrites the URL hash.

label: close
desc: Empties the stack, notifies subscribers, and clears the URL hash.

label: refresh
desc: Re-notifies subscribers without changing the stack. Used after the store loads new chunks so a hash-restored view repaints with real data.

label: panelRouter
desc: Default Router instance shared across the SPA.

label: RefsView
desc: View that lists docs or flows referencing a given node. Instantiated per navigation with nodeId, kind, and deps.

label: renderAccordion
desc: Builds the docs/flows accordion HTML.

label: wireRefs
desc: Attaches hover, click and prefetch handlers to every a.tskb-ref under a root element.

label: NodeRefHooks
desc: Callbacks the host gives to panel views for graph lookups and navigation side effects.

label: BaseNodeRenderer
desc: Default node component. Draws any node as a card.

label: createNodeRenderer
desc: Builds a node renderer wired with the SPA's interaction callbacks.

label: buildStructureLinks
desc: Derives parent–child links from the visible nodes.

label: renderStructureEdges
desc: Draws parent–child edges on the canvas.

label: renderBoundaryGroups
desc: Draws an outline around the nodes that belong to each boundary.

label: computeLayout
desc: Computes positions for all visible nodes.

label: ChunkLoader
desc: Loads chunks for the SPA.

label: GraphStore
desc: Holds loaded graph data and notifies subscribers when it changes.

label: serveExplorer
desc: Starts the explorer HTTP server.

label: explore
desc: Runs the `tskb explore` command — serves the SPA, or exports it as static files with `--export`.

label: exportExplorer
desc: Exports the explorer to a folder that opens from the file system.

label: ExplorerApp
desc: Top-level controller for the explorer SPA.

label: mount
desc: Boots the SPA. Call once on startup.

label: setupCanvas
desc: Creates the SVG layers and wires zoom and pan.

label: setupTooltips
desc: Sets up the hover tooltip and the code preview popup.

label: setupRenderer
desc: Builds the node renderer with its interaction callbacks.

label: setupSearch
desc: Creates the search Web Worker and wires button clicks to send queries and receive ranked matchIds.

label: loadInitialData
desc: Loads the meta chunk and shows or hides the global spinner.

label: render
desc: Re-runs the render loop. Computes state, then draws.

label: computeRenderState
desc: Pure function that produces the render state from store, layout, and search query.

label: onExpand
desc: Handles expand and collapse for folders and modules.

label: onTraceLinks
desc: Hook for tracing edges from a node. Stub today.

label: collapseDescendants
desc: Collapses a folder and everything inside it.

label: prefetchNodeChunk
desc: Loads the chunks needed to resolve a node, without expanding it on the canvas.

label: createProgram
desc: Builds a TypeScript Program for static analysis. Does not emit output.

label: extractRegistry
desc: Extracts the registry from TypeScript types.

label: ExtractedRegistry
desc: The registry data after extraction.

label: extractModuleMorphology
desc: Extracts a module's public exports and renders short code stubs for them.

label: extractModuleImports
desc: Extracts a module's import statements.

label: buildGraph
desc: Builds the knowledge graph from extracted registry and docs.

label: KnowledgeGraph
desc: The full graph: nodes, edges, and metadata.

label: FlowNode
desc: Graph node for a Flow.

label: FlowStep
desc: One step inside a Flow.

label: writeSplitGraph
desc: Writes the graph to `.tskb/graph/` as separate JSON files, one per node type.

label: extractDocs
desc: Extracts every `.tskb.tsx` file matched by the glob.

label: ExtractedDoc
desc: The data extracted from one doc file.

label: extractFromTsxFile
desc: Extracts one `.tskb.tsx` file.

label: buildRefMap
desc: Maps each `const` in a doc file to the registry node it points to.

label: JsxExtractor
desc: Walks the JSX tree of a doc file and collects content, refs, relations, and flows.

label: buildRefLink
desc: Builds the HTML anchor for one inline reference.

label: resolveNodeMeta
desc: Looks up a node id in the registry and returns its type and display name.

label: ExtractConfig
desc: Config the build command takes: glob pattern and tsconfig path.

label: resolveNode
desc: Resolves an ID or path to a node in the graph.

label: getNodeEdges
desc: Returns a node's incoming and outgoing edges.

label: findReferencingDocs
desc: Returns the docs that reference a given node.

label: findParent
desc: Returns the parent folder or module of a node.

label: findAllNodesById
desc: Finds every node sharing the same ID across types.

label: loadGraph
desc: Reads the split graph from `.tskb/graph/`. Pass a list of node types to load only what you need.

label: library
desc: The tskb package itself — its CLI, JSX runtime, and types for writing docs.

label: cli
desc: The command-line tool shipped by the package. Run as `tskb` after install. Builds the graph, runs queries, opens the explorer.

label: jsxRuntime
desc: A custom JSX runtime that turns .tskb.tsx files into a knowledge graph instead of DOM. Picked up by the docs tsconfig via `jsxImportSource: "tskb"`.

label: registry
desc: The global `namespace tskb` where Folders, Modules, Exports, and Terms are declared. All .tskb.tsx files share one registry — declarations merge across files.

label: graph
desc: The output of the build: every node (folder, module, export, term, doc, flow) and the edges between them. Written to `.tskb/graph/` as separate JSON files, one per node type.

label: knowledgeChunk
desc: A JSON piece of the graph the SPA loads on demand. The meta chunk holds top-level info; one folder chunk per folder holds its contents.

label: explorerLane
desc: A horizontal band on the explorer canvas. Lanes group different kinds of nodes side by side.

label: nodeComponent
desc: The interface a node type implements to render itself on the canvas.

label: lruChunkCache
desc: A bounded cache inside the SPA. Keeps recently loaded chunks; drops the oldest when full.

label: explorerStaticExport
desc: A self-contained explorer folder that opens directly from the file system, no server needed.

label: ghostNode
desc: A placeholder node for a folder or file that exists on disk but isn't declared in the graph. Drawn as a faded card.

label: searchIndexChunk
desc: A flat array of all graph nodes with just the fields needed for search (id, type, label, description, path). Served at /chunks/search-index.json and fetched once by the search worker.

label: tsProgram
desc: A TypeScript compiler `Program` built from source files and a tsconfig. tskb uses it to read the AST, types, and symbols of your code — without emitting JavaScript.

label: ast
desc: Abstract Syntax Tree. TypeScript's tree shape of source code.

label: declarationMerging
desc: TypeScript's rule that merges declarations with the same name. Lets the registry be split across files.

label: typeChecker
desc: TypeScript's component that resolves types and gives type info for symbols.

label: literalType
desc: A type that represents one specific value, like the string `'hello'`. Used to read constants out of type definitions.

label: jsxElement
desc:
A JSX node in the source tree, like `` or `

`.

label: jsxExpression
desc: A `{ ... }` expression embedded inside JSX.

label: typeAssertion
desc: TypeScript's `as` syntax. tskb uses it to point a `ref` at a registry entry: `ref as tskb.Modules['X']`.

label: depthFirstTraversal
desc: Tree traversal that follows each branch to its end before moving to the next. Used to walk JSX in order.

label: constantReferencesMap
desc: A lookup from each `const` in a doc file to the registry node it points to.

label: refCategoryMap
desc: A lookup from a JSX tag name to the registry category it belongs to.

label: globPattern
desc: A file-matching pattern like `**/*.tskb.tsx`. Used to pick which doc files to build.

label: tskbOutputDir
desc: The `.tskb/` folder. Holds the build output: a `graph/` directory with per-type JSON files.

label: searchResult
desc: A match returned by `tskb search`. A node plus a score from 0 to 1; higher means a better match.

label: resolvedVia
desc: How `resolveNode` found a match: by exact ID, by path, or by walking up to the nearest parent folder.

label: md
desc: The main npm README.md for the tskb package

label: vitest
desc: The test runner.

label: npm
desc: npm package registry where tskb is published. The package includes the CLI binary, library entry point, JSX runtime, and pre-built explorer SPA assets.

label: d3
desc: Data-visualisation library used for layout, zoom, and SVG drawing.

label: vite
desc: Build tool that bundles the explorer SPA.

label: typescript
desc: TypeScript compiler API (the 'typescript' npm package). Provides the AST, type checker, and symbol resolution used throughout registry extraction and documentation parsing.

label: e2e-test-execution
desc: Developer runs `npm test`: Vitest loads config, global setup builds the fixture graph, test files exercise every CLI command, teardown cleans output
content: vitest.config discovers test files, registers global setup tests.global-setup builds fixture graph once via tskb build tests.helpers provides CLI runners and graph loader to all test files tests.global-setup teardown cleans .tskb/ output directory

label: init-scaffolding-test
desc: `init.test.ts` runs: copies fixture to a temp dir, invokes `tskb init`, asserts the generated files
content: tests.init orchestrates the test: arranges temp dir, runs init, asserts output tests.helpers copies fixture src/ into the temp directory cli.build runs tskb init --yes in the temp directory tests.init asserts scaffolded files: tsconfig, starter doc, scripts, AI dirs

label: build-pipeline
desc: The tskb build process: source files through extraction to knowledge graph outputs
content: cli.build Orchestrates the pipeline: file discovery, extraction, graph building, output generation extractRegistry Scans TypeScript AST for global tskb namespace declarations (Folders, Modules, Exports, Terms) extractDocs Receives completed registry; traverses JSX trees to extract content, references, relations, and flows — embeds data-node-display in reference links using registry-resolved paths buildGraph Merges registry and docs into a unified graph with nodes, edges, and hierarchy writeSplitGraph Writes the graph to .tskb/graph/ — one JSON file per node type plus a search index

label: graph-to-chunks-transform
desc: The explorer asks transformGraph to chunk a KnowledgeGraph for delivery: edges are indexed, the meta chunk is built, folder chunks are walked, ghosts are filled in, then counts and ordering are finalised
content: explorer.transformGraph entry point: instantiates the transformer, indexes edges by source and target, then runs the phases below explorer.transform builds the meta chunk: root folder, top folders, docs, flows, terms, externals, and cross-lane edges explorer.transform recursively builds one folder chunk per folder with its direct modules, exports, sub-folders, and import edges (split into internal vs external) explorer.transform fills in ghost intermediary folders for path gaps between a declared folder and its modules, then injects ghost nodes for undeclared files and folders found by the scanner explorer.transform patches folder child counts so the SPA can show expand affordances, sorts chunk contents alphabetically, and builds the parent-of map

label: router-setup
desc: Boot wiring: panel shell subscribes, view factory is registered for hash restore, hash sync starts
content: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.mount ExplorerApp.mount() runs the panel-router setup block after canvas and renderer are ready explorer.spa.DocPanel new DocPanel(router): shell subscribes so every stack change re-renders the panel explorer.spa.Router.registerView registerView(RefsView, deps): binds deps into a factory closure keyed by RefsView.prefix explorer.spa.Router.init init({ syncHash: true }): starts hashchange listening and restores any view in the current hash explorer.spa.router If location.hash is non-empty, restoreFromHash() calls the factory to rebuild the view explorer.spa.DocPanel onViewChange fires; header and body are rendered by the restored view

label: router-push
desc: Forward navigation: a chip click constructs a view, pushes it, the shell renders it, and the URL hash is updated
content: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp onChipClick(node, 'docs' | 'flows'): constructs new RefsView(nodeId, kind, deps) explorer.spa.Router.push router.push(view): identical-route guard skips dupes; otherwise appends to the stack explorer.spa.router notify() fans out the new top-of-stack and canGoBack to every subscriber explorer.spa.DocPanel onViewChange clears title/body, calls view.renderHeader(title, ctx) then view.renderBody(body) explorer.spa.RefsView renderHeader and renderBody write HTML then call wireRefs on their own elements explorer.spa.router writeHash() writes #/<route> to location.hash; the writingHash flag suppresses the echoed hashchange

label: router-hash-restore
desc: Browser back/forward or external hash change rebuilds the view from the URL
content: explorer.spa.router hashchange fires; if writingHash is set the event is our own echo and is ignored explorer.spa.router restoreFromHash() strips the leading '#/'; an empty hash empties the stack and notifies explorer.spa.router parseRoute(raw) splits the prefix, looks up the registered factory, and calls it with the rest explorer.spa.RefsView RefsView.parse(rest, deps) decodes the route and returns a fresh view instance (or null for malformed routes) explorer.spa.router Existing stack is drained (each onLeave fired) and replaced with the single restored view explorer.spa.DocPanel onViewChange paints the restored view; getNode may return undefined if the chunk hasn't loaded yet

label: router-refresh-after-load
desc: A hash-restored view that painted with placeholder labels repaints once the meta chunk arrives
content: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.loadInitialData loadInitialData() awaits the meta chunk and writes it to the store explorer.spa.Router.refresh router.refresh() re-notifies subscribers without changing the stack explorer.spa.router notify() fans out the same top-of-stack view to every subscriber explorer.spa.DocPanel onViewChange runs again; the view re-queries deps.getNode() and now resolves real labels

label: explorer-serve-flow
desc: Developer runs `tskb explore`: the CLI loads the graph, transforms it into chunks, starts an HTTP server, and the browser loads the SPA and fetches chunks on demand
content: explorer.explore loads the graph file and routes to the serve path explorer.transformGraph converts the KnowledgeGraph into a MetaChunk and one FolderChunk per folder, held in memory explorer.serveExplorer starts the HTTP server: chunk routes serve the in-memory chunks as JSON; everything else serves the SPA assets explorer.spa.main ExplorerApp mounts on page load: sets up canvas, renderer, and search, then fetches the meta chunk explorer.spa.lane-engine positions all visible nodes across the three lanes explorer.spa.node-base D3 enter/update renders node cards into SVG groups

label: explorer-export-flow
desc: Developer runs the explorer export command: the graph file is read, chunks are transformed in memory, then the pre-built SPA and chunk files are written to the output directory
content: explorer.explore reads the graph file and resolves the output directory from the export flag explorer.transformGraph converts KnowledgeGraph into a MetaChunk plus one FolderChunk per folder, held in memory explorer.exportExplorer copies the pre-built SPA into the output directory and writes all chunk JSON files alongside it

label: explorer-app-boot
desc: ExplorerApp.mount(): four setup phases then meta fetch that triggers the first render
content: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp new ExplorerApp().mount() called on page load explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.setupCanvas Appends zoom-layer → lane-bg-layer / boundary-layer / edge-layer / node-layer; configures d3.zoom with tooltip transform propagation explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.setupTooltips mountNodeTooltip() and mountCodeTooltip() attach DOM tooltip elements anchored to the SVG explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.setupRenderer createNodeRenderer() wires onExpand / onSelect / onTraceLinks / hasChildren / code preview callbacks to ExplorerApp instance methods explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.setupSearch Creates search Web Worker; button click posts query to worker; worker results update matchIds and call render() explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.loadInitialData loader.load('meta') fetches /chunks/meta.json; global spinner shown while pending explorer.spa.store store.loadMeta() stores the chunk and notifies the render subscriber explorer.spa.lane-engine First render(): computeLayout positions top-folder nodes across the Structure lane

label: explorer-app-render
desc: A store update or interaction calls ExplorerApp.render(): layout, edges, D3 join, search dim
content: explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.render triggered by store subscription or a direct call from an interaction handler explorer.spa.computeLayout computeLayout(store, expanded): builds d3.hierarchy from visible tree, runs d3.tree for left-to-right positions. Result cached until layoutDirty is set. explorer.spa.computeRenderState computeRenderState(store, layout, matchIds): derives allNodes, canvasW, structureLinks, relationLinks. matchIds comes from the search worker. Pure — no D3. explorer.spa.edge-renderer renderLaneBands(): draws labeled background bands for Structure and Externals lanes explorer.spa.buildStructureLinks buildStructureLinks(structureNodes): derives parent→child StructureLink pairs from parentId fields explorer.spa.renderStructureEdges renderStructureEdges(): redraws cubic-bezier SVG paths; ghost links get dashed stroke explorer.spa.node-base D3 enter/update/exit: new node cards appended, merged nodes repositioned, removed nodes fade out; search dim sets 0.15 on non-matching nodes

label: explorer-app-expand
desc: ExplorerApp.onExpand(): fetch chunk if needed, update expanded set, re-render
content: explorer.spa.node-base User clicks + button; BaseNodeRenderer fires the onExpand callback explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.onExpand If already expanded: collapseDescendants() clears the subtree, calls render() explorer.spa.showNodeSpinner If chunk not cached: showNodeSpinner() appends animated SVG ring next to the node explorer.spa.loader loader.load('folder', id): cache hit or fetches /chunks/folder-{sanitizedId}.json explorer.spa.removeNodeSpinner removeNodeSpinner() called in finally — always removes spinner whether fetch succeeded or failed explorer.spa.store store.loadFolderChunk() stores chunk and notifies subscribers explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.render render() called after expanded set update — new nodes enter, layout recomputed

label: static-analysis
desc: `tskb build` invokes createProgram to set up TypeScript static analysis, then hands the Program to extraction and graph assembly
content: ts.createProgram creates Program from tsconfig + source files extraction.registry walks AST for Folders, Modules, Terms, Exports extraction.documentation parses JSX trees for content and references graph.builder assembles nodes and edges into knowledge graph

label: module-morphology-extraction
desc: During `tskb build`, extractRegistry enriches every Module declaration with its public API: exports, imports, and type stubs
content: extractRegistry collects Module declarations from tskb namespace extractModuleMorphology resolves source file, classifies exports, renders code stubs extractModuleImports walks import declarations for symbols and paths graph.builder assembles enriched ModuleNode with edges

label: docs-extraction-pipeline
desc: Single .tskb.tsx file through extraction: constant map → JSX traversal → HTML + references
content: extractDocs Iterates Program source files; dispatches matched .tsx files to extractFromTsxFile extractFromTsxFile Builds constant ref map, finds default export, instantiates JsxExtractor buildRefMap Walks all variable declarations; maps const names → {category, name} via type-assertion parsing extraction.registry Passed in as optional param; queried by buildRefLink to pre-compute data-node-display values JsxExtractor Depth-first JSX traversal via visitElement dispatch: structural tags → HTML, XxxRef / inline refs → buildRefLink; Flow and Relation elements validated and collected buildRefLink Emits <a class=tskb-ref data-node-id data-node-type data-node-display>; pushes id into refs sub-array resolveNodeMeta Called per Flow step: probes all registry maps to return {nodeType, display} for the step anchor ExtractedDoc Returned: filePath, HTML content, references arrays, flows[], relations[] graph.builder Consumes ExtractedDoc: creates DocNode + FlowNode entries and references/flow-step edges

label: tsx
desc: What's the typical workflow for setting up tskb in a repo?
content: Adopting tskb in a project follows a small fixed sequence: configure, declare, write, build. Create a docs/ folder for .tskb.tsx files. Add a tsconfig like sample.tsconfig.json pointing at the repo root with jsx: "react-jsx" and jsxImportSource: "tskb" . Declare structural elements (Folders, Modules, Exports, Terms, Files) using global namespace augmentation. Use typeof import() for type-safe refe

label: tsx
desc: How is the tskb CLI validated end-to-end?
content: E2E Test Infrastructure The test suite ( tests ) validates the entire tskb CLI by running it as a subprocess against a realistic fixture project. Tests use Vitest and spawn the built CLI binary via Node's execFileSync, asserting on stdout and generated artifacts. Developer runs `npm test`: Vitest loads config, global setup builds the fixture graph, test files exercise every CLI command, teardown c

label: tsx
desc: What primitives does tskb provide for writing documentation?
content: The library exports a small set of registry primitives for declaring structural elements, plus JSX components from Jsx.runtime.js for writing the prose around them. Folder — logical groupings in the codebase (features, layers, packages). Module — concrete code units (source files). Export — type-safe reference to a named export. Uses typeof import() so the compiler validates the symbol exists. Ter

label: tsx
desc: What is tskb and what does this package contain?
content: tskb A TypeScript DSL for type-safe architectural documentation. TSKB.Package.Root contains the library, CLI, and pre-built explorer SPA. Its package.json declares a tskb bin that runs the cli , and npm.README.md is the public-facing readme. Two main entry modules sit alongside the cli : Main.index.js (the library API) and Jsx.runtime.js (the JSX runtime, jsxRuntime ). The tskb build process: sour

label: tsx
desc: What does the runtime module provide and what does it not do?
content: Runtime Located in tskb.runtime . Contains registry type definitions and JSX primitives - no actual runtime execution. Modules runtime.jsx : JSX runtime with jsx.Doc (requires explains prop and optional priority via DocPriority : essential, constraint, or supplementary). Includes heading/paragraph/list components, jsx.Adr for Architecture Decision Records, and jsx.ref for type-safe registry refere

label: tsx
desc: How does the KnowledgeGraph become the chunks the explorer SPA loads?
content: Graph Transform explorer.transform is the single step between the raw knowledge graph and what the browser receives. explorer.transformGraph is implemented as a class ( GraphToExplorerTransformer ) whose transform() method orchestrates all steps — edge indexing, meta assembly, folder chunk recursion, ghost injection, and child count patching. Output shape The output is ExplorerChunks : a MetaChunk

label: tsx
desc: What are the SPA's main layers and how do they fit together?
content: Explorer SPA The SPA is built with Vite (D3 in a separate vendor chunk) and lives in the explorer-app/ directory. It is pre-built into dist/explorer/ and shipped inside the tskb npm package. explorer.spa.main is the entry point that bootstraps the canvas, wires all interaction handlers, and drives the render loop. The SPA is split into five layers, each documented separately: Data layer ( explorer

label: tsx
desc: How do tooltips and spinners track canvas nodes as they pan and zoom?
content: Tooltips and spinners Spinners explorer.spa.spinner provides a full-screen global spinner (shown while meta.json loads) and a per-node inline spinner: explorer.spa.showNodeSpinner appends an animated SVG ring at the node's canvas position; explorer.spa.removeNodeSpinner removes it in the finally block after the chunk fetch settles. Hover and code tooltips explorer.spa.node-tooltip appears on hover

label: tsx
desc: How does the doc panel host and route addressable views?
content: Doc panel and router Panel host explorer.spa.DocPanel in explorer.spa.doc-panel is the detail side panel. It is a thin shell — it subscribes to explorer.spa.panelRouter and, on each stack change, clears its header and body slots then delegates rendering entirely to the active View . It owns no view-specific markup, no deps, and no wiring logic. Router and views explorer.spa.Router maintains a stac

label: tsx
desc: How are nodes, edges, and boundaries rendered on the canvas?
content: Nodes, edges, and boundaries Node components explorer.spa.node-index is the extension point for node rendering: explorer.spa.createNodeRenderer accepts all interaction callbacks and returns a nodeComponent . Currently always delegates to explorer.spa.BaseNodeRenderer ; add a type-dispatch switch here when a node type needs its own renderer. explorer.spa.node-base defines the nodeComponent interfac

label: tsx
desc: How does the lane engine arrange nodes into three lanes on the canvas?
content: Three-lane layout explorer.spa.lane-engine computes all SVG positions in a single pass. The canvas has three explorerLane s stacked vertically: Structure — builds a d3.hierarchy from the visible tree (root → expanded folders → modules → exports, including sub-folder nesting). Runs d3.tree().nodeSize() for left-to-right positioning. Collapsed folders with _childCount > 0 inject ghost placeholder

label: tsx
desc: How does the SPA load and cache graph chunks?
content: Data layer explorer.spa.chunk-types defines the chunk discriminated union ( MetaChunk | FolderChunk ) and the ChunkRegistry map that keys chunk kinds to their types. Adding a new chunk kind means extending this file only. explorer.spa.loader provides explorer.spa.ChunkLoader : a generic load(kind, ...args) whose return type is inferred from ChunkRegistry[K] . It deduplicates in-flight requests and

label: tsx
desc: How does the explorer HTTP server serve chunks and SPA assets?
content: Explorer Server explorer.serveExplorer in explorer.server starts a Node built-in http server — no external dependency. It runs the graph transform once at startup, serializes every knowledgeChunk to JSON, and keeps all of them in a Map<string, string> for the lifetime of the process. Subsequent chunk requests are pure string writes with no re-serialization. Request routing Two routes are han

label: tsx
desc: How does the explorer export to a static directory that opens from file://?
content: Explorer Static Export explorer.exportExplorer in explorer.export produces a explorerStaticExport : a directory containing the pre-built SPA ( index.html + hashed JS/CSS assets) and all knowledgeChunk JSON files written to a chunks/ sub-directory. Output layout The default output directory is .tskb/explorer/ . The structure mirrors what the server serves dynamically: index.html , assets/ — Vite-bu

label: tsx
desc: What is the tskb explorer and how does its data flow from CLI to browser?
content: Explorer The explorer is a D3-powered SPA that renders the knowledge graph as a visual, interactive diagram. It opens via tskb explore (local HTTP server) or exports to a self-contained static folder via tskb explore --export . The implementation splits into two layers: the CLI-side layer in tskb.explorer.core (graph transform, HTTP server, static export) and the Vite SPA in tskb.explorer.app (D3

label: tsx
desc: How does the ExplorerApp class boot, render, and respond to interaction?
content: ExplorerApp explorer.spa.ExplorerApp in explorer.spa.main is the single top-level controller for the explorer SPA. All canvas layers, UI state, and callbacks are owned by the class instance. The only public surface is explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.mount — everything else is private, wired internally via callbacks. Boot sequence explorer.spa.ExplorerApp.mount runs four synchronous setup methods then awa

label: tsx
desc: How does tskb create a TypeScript Program for static analysis without compiling?
content: TypeScript Program Lives under tskb.core as the typescript area. typescript.program exports ts.createProgram which creates a tsProgram for analyzing code without compilation (noEmit=true). Reads and parses tsconfig.json for compiler options Validates TypeScript errors in specified files Returns Program with AST, type checker, and symbol resolution `tskb build` invokes createProgram to set up TypeS

label: tsx
desc: How does tskb extract Folders, Modules, Exports, and Terms from the TypeScript AST?
content: Registry Extraction extractRegistry walks the TypeScript AST for registry declarations, then enriches each module with its public API shape via extraction.module-morphology . During `tskb build`, extractRegistry enriches every Module declaration with its public API: exports, imports, and type stubs 1 extractRegistry collects Module declarations from tskb namespace 2 extractModuleMorphology resolve

label: tsx
desc: How is the knowledge graph modeled and assembled from extracted data?
content: Graph Module Located in core.extraction.graph . Takes extracted registry and documentation, produces a KnowledgeGraph . Modules graph.types : Defines the graph data model. Node types: FolderNode, ModuleNode, TermNode, ExportNode, FlowNode , DocNode. Edge types: references, belongs-to, contains, related-to, flow-step. Top-level structure: KnowledgeGraph with nodes, edges, and metadata. graph.builde

label: tsx
desc: How does tskb turn .tskb.tsx files into HTML, references, and graph edges?
content: Documentation Extraction extraction.documentation is the second extraction pass in the build pipeline. extractDocs receives the TypeScript Program and the extraction.registry output, and produces an ExtractedDoc for each .tskb.tsx file. Each result carries the HTML content string, the reference arrays used to build graph edges, and any embedded jsx.Flow and declarations. Two-pass strategy per file

label: tsx
desc: What test coverage is required for new functionality in the tskb package?
content: Any new behaviour added to the tskb package must ship with E2E coverage in tests . The suite runs the built CLI as a subprocess against the fixture project at tests.e2e.fixture via vitest , so changes are validated against a realistic shape rather than mocks. Tests must pass before a change is merged or the package is published. Pick the test file that owns the surface you changed: New or modified

label: tsx
desc: How are the .claude/skills/ files maintained?
content: Every file under `.claude/skills/tskb*/` — both `SKILL.md` and any `references/*.md` — is generated by the docs build. Hand edits to those files are overwritten on the next run, so treat the generated tree as build output, not source. The source of truth lives in two modules. cli.utils.content-builder owns the markdown bodies: skill prose, the reference-file contents, and the Copilot instructions.

label: tsx
desc: When must the npm README.md be updated?
content: The package in TSKB.Package.Root is published to npm. npm.README.md is the primary documentation users see on the registry. When structural changes are made to the consumer-facing surface — tskb.cli commands, runtime.jsx components, runtime.registry , runtime primitives - $ tskb.runtime , type interfaces, build output format, or generated artifacts ( cli.utils.skill-generator , cli.utils.copilot-i

label: tsx
desc: How are explorer panel views structured?
content: Every panel state in the explorer is a View class held in the explorer.spa.router stack. explorer.spa.doc-panel renders whichever view is on top — it has no view-specific markup of its own. New panel surfaces (docs list, settings page, flow inspector) must be added as Views, not as new methods on the panel or on explorer.spa.Router . A view class must: Live in tskb.explorer.app.router.views and im

label: tsx
desc: How does the CLI route log output and what controls verbosity?
content: CLI Logger cli.utils.logger is a module-level singleton that routes all CLI output to stderr, keeping stdout clean for query command results. Design All log output goes to stderr via process.stderr.write — never stdout Configured once at startup in cli.index via configure( { verbose } ), driven by the --verbose CLI flag Log functions: info() always prints, verbose() only when --verbose is set, err

label: tsx
desc: CLI structure: commands (init, build, search, pick, context, ls, docs, flows) and utils (output generators, content builder, logger)
content: CLI Located in tskb.cli . Entry point: cli.index — parses arguments, routes to command handlers. Commands In tskb.cli.commands : cli.commands.init : Interactive scaffolder — creates docs folder, tsconfig, starter doc, build script cli.commands.build : Full pipeline — files → TypeScript program → extraction → graph → outputs cli.commands.search : Fuzzy search across all node types, returns ranked J
